parameters.content:
## Ingestion
Polls signal-rest-api every 5s and accepts webhook POSTs. Normalizes both sources into a unified message format with sender, content, timestamp, and attachments.

## Command Parser
Extracts flags (_translate, _tts, _audio) from message text. Routes /help commands to the help response flow.

## Identity & Content Key
Resolves sender UUID to name/preferences via PostgreSQL. Normalizes content URLs into cache keys. Validates URL support (YouTube, web).

## Cache & Routing
Checks PostgreSQL cache for existing summaries. Serves cached results instantly or queues new jobs for Signal_Worker. Braindumps get a separate queue path.

## Error Handling
Catches processing errors, formats user-friendly messages, and sends them back via Signal REST API.

nodes.name: Schedule Trigger
nodes.notes: Polls every 5 seconds for new Signal messages.

nodes.name: Poll Signal API
nodes.notes: Fetches pending messages from signal-cli REST API every 5 seconds.

nodes.name: Filter Empty
nodes.notes: Skips processing when Signal API returns empty array. Most polls return nothing.

nodes.name: Split Messages
nodes.notes: Processes Signal messages one at a time through the pipeline.

nodes.name: Webhook Trigger
nodes.notes: Receives direct HTTP POST requests from bookmarklet or shortcuts.

nodes.name: Normalize Input
nodes.notes: Merges Signal messages and webhook payloads into one unified format.

nodes.name: Command Parser
nodes.notes: Extracts command flags (!deep, !quick, !translate, etc.) and URL from message text.

nodes.name: Help Check
nodes.notes: help command? yes = send help text, no = continue pipeline

nodes.name: Help Response
nodes.notes: Returns command list with usage examples formatted for Signal.

nodes.name: Send Help
nodes.notes: Sends help text back to the sender via Signal API.

parameters.query: INSERT INTO pipeline_users (name, role, signal_uuid) VALUES ('Unknown', 'user', '{{ $('Command Parser').first().json._senderUuid }}') ON CONFLICT (signal_uuid) DO UPDATE SET name = pipeline_users.name RETURNING id, name, role;

nodes.name: Resolve Identity
nodes.notes: Looks up or creates the sender in pipeline_users. Returns user ID, role, name.

nodes.name: Normalize Content Key
nodes.notes: Extracts stable content key from URL for cache lookups. YouTube = video ID, web = host+path.

nodes.name: Has URL?
nodes.notes: message has a URL? yes = content pipeline, no = braindump/voice path

parameters.query: SELECT cc.content_key, cc.content_type, cc.title, cc.source_url, cc.transcript_path, cc.vault_path, cc.processed_at, cc.processed_by, cv.result AS _cachedSummary, cv.variant_type AS _cachedVariant FROM content_cache cc LEFT JOIN content_variants cv ON cc.content_key = cv.content_key AND cv.variant_type = 'summary' WHERE cc.content_key = '{{ $('Normalize Content Key').first().json._contentKey }}' LIMIT 1;

nodes.name: Cache Check
nodes.notes: Queries content_cache + content_variants for existing processed results.

nodes.name: Check Cache Result
nodes.notes: Checks if Cache Check returned a cached row. Sets _hasCacheHit flag.

nodes.name: Serve Cached
nodes.notes: Formats cached summary for Signal delivery. Admin gets truncated + vault link.

nodes.name: Send Cache Response
nodes.notes: Sends cached summary back to sender via Signal API.

nodes.name: Queue Job
nodes.notes: Inserts URL-based processing job into pipeline_queue.

nodes.name: Send Ack
nodes.notes: Builds acknowledgment message. Admin gets clickable link, user gets confirmation.

nodes.name: Send Ack Message
nodes.notes: Sends queue acknowledgment back to sender via Signal API.

nodes.name: Error Handler
nodes.notes: Catches pipeline failures, formats user-friendly error for Signal reply.

nodes.name: Send Error Msg
nodes.notes: Sends error message back to user via Signal.

nodes.name: Has Cache?
nodes.notes: cached result found? yes = serve immediately, no = queue for processing

nodes.name: Supported Type?
nodes.notes: known content type? yes = continue, no = send unsupported message

nodes.name: Unsupported Response
nodes.notes: Builds 'coming soon' reply for Spotify/Tidal and empty message handling.

nodes.name: Send Unsupported Msg
nodes.notes: Sends 'unsupported content type' reply via Signal.

nodes.name: Queue Braindump
nodes.notes: Inserts braindump/voice job into pipeline_queue for the Worker to pick up.

nodes.name: Braindump Ack
nodes.notes: Builds ack message for braindump/voice note submissions.

nodes.name: Send Braindump Ack
nodes.notes: Sends braindump/voice note acknowledgment via Signal API.

parameters.content:
# Signal Ingestion

## Front door for the STMNA Signal pipeline. Polls signal-rest-api every 5s and accepts webhook POSTs. Normalizes messages, parses commands, resolves sender identity, checks content cache, and either serves cached results or queues jobs for Signal_Worker.

## Trigger: Schedule (5s poll) + Webhook POST
## Output: Cached responses via Signal, or queued jobs in pipeline_queue

## Credential
**Postgres Signal**
Resolves sender UUID to user profile.

## Credential
**Postgres Signal**
Checks pipeline_cache for existing summaries.

## Credential
**Postgres Signal**
Inserts new processing jobs into pipeline_queue.

## Credential
**Postgres Signal**
Queues braindump-type content separately.

## Config
**Signal REST API**
`YOUR_HOST_IP:8082` — must replace with your signal-cli-rest-api endpoint.

**Signal REST API**
`YOUR_HOST_IP:8082` — must replace with your signal-cli-rest-api endpoint.

## User Config
**Admin UUID**
`ADMIN_UUID` constant. Set to your admin Signal UUID.
**Forgejo URL**
`forgejoBase` URL for vault file links in cached responses.

## User Config

## User Config
**Admin UUID**
`ADMIN_UUID` constant. Set to your admin Signal UUID.

## User Config
**Admin UUID**
`YOUR_UUID` fallback used for webhook/direct senders. Replace with your admin Signal UUID.

## User Config
**Admin UUID**
`YOUR_UUID` fallback for _senderUuid in error replies. Replace with your admin Signal UUID.
